rows.definition: cmd/sessionbench is the Go benchmark command that measures the net value-add of fak's fused agent kernel over a synthetic multi-agent, multi-turn session workload (its prefillTokens A/B/C formula).
rows.distinction: Not a live runtime `session`: it is an offline measurement harness that drives many synthetic sessions to compute a value stack, never a served request.

rows.definition: DurabilitySession is the ctxplan/ctxmmu durability-tier constant ("session") that marks a stored context span as true only for the current session's lifetime.
rows.distinction: Not the runtime `session` object: it is a retention-class label on a span, ranking above DurabilityTurn and below DurabilityBounded/DurabilityDurable.

rows.definition: model.BatchSession decodes B independent user sequences in lockstep over one shared weight stream (continuous-batching decode), built via NewBatchSession.
rows.distinction: Not a single-user model.Session nor the gateway DRIVE-state session record: it is the multi-lane decode engine that holds one KV cache per lane.

rows.definition: decideSession is the host-injected request-boundary hook that applies session.Table.Decide so each served model request honors run-state, turn/token/context budget, and pace before the upstream call runs.
rows.distinction: Not observeSession (read-only): decideSession MUTATES the drive table (debits TurnsLeft) and returns a proceed/refuse verdict.

rows.definition: observeSession is the read side of the /v1/fak/session control surface, returning one served session's current DRIVE state (run-state, budget, pace) without mutating the table.
rows.distinction: Not decideSession (the mutating boundary): observeSession only snapshots state and an unseen trace reads the table's safe Running default.

rows.definition: debitSession is the host hook that records a served request's post-response token usage (output + context) into the drive table so the next Decide sees budget exhaustion.
rows.distinction: Not decideSession (the pre-turn gate): debitSession runs AFTER the planner returns and only accounts usage, it never refuses.

rows.definition: model.NewBackendSession starts a single-user model.Session whose per-token forward path runs through an injected compute HAL backend (CUDA/Vulkan/Metal device).
rows.distinction: Not BatchSession (multi-lane decode): this is one fresh session with an empty KV whose math is offloaded to a hardware backend rather than the CPU reference path.

rows.definition: servedSessionTurn is the gateway's internal per-request record (traceID, resolved SessionState, maxTokens, minGapMs) produced when the session gate admits one proxied model request.
rows.distinction: Not the public session-turn DTO: it is an unexported gateway value carrying the pace caps for THIS request, consumed by debitServedSessionTurn.

rows.definition: Server.beginServedSessionTurn applies the live session gate to one proxied model request, calling the wired DecideSession (or the ObserveSession fallback) and enforcing the pace min-gap before the turn runs.
rows.distinction: Not the servedSessionTurn struct it returns: this is the method that PRODUCES that record and decides proceed/refuse/cancel.

rows.definition: Server.debitServedSessionTurn reports a just-served model request's provider usage back through the injected DebitSession hook so output/context budgets are debited.
rows.distinction: Not beginServedSessionTurn (the pre-turn admit): this is the post-turn accounting half of the same boundary.

rows.definition: resetServedSessionOnBudget is the host-owned ResetOnBudgetFunc the gateway calls after a context-budget drain to distill a carryover seed, re-arm the continuation trace with a fresh context budget, and let the live request continue transparently.
rows.distinction: Not the SessionResetDirective wire payload: this is the server-side closure that performs the human-like reset (Recontinue), not the JSON handoff a supervisor reads.

rows.definition: steerSession enqueues an operator steer onto the process-global a2achan bus (keyed by the session trace) so a running detached session can splice the input at its next turn boundary.
rows.distinction: Not controlSession (DRIVE-state verbs): steerSession injects free-text guidance over the agent bus, subject to the a2achan default-deny floor, not a run/budget/pace mutation.

rows.definition: controlSession is the write side of /v1/fak/session, applying one DRIVE control verb (run|budget|pace|priority) to a live session's state with optional if_rev optimistic-concurrency.
rows.distinction: Not observeSession (read side): controlSession mutates the table and maps a CAS/terminal refusal to 409 and a malformed verb to 400.

rows.definition: cdb.IngestSession parses a Claude Code session JSONL transcript at a path into a recall.Recorder, applying the ctxmmu write-time seal/quarantine decision per tool result.
rows.distinction: Not a live runtime session: it is an offline transcript importer producing a recorder for recall/contextq, never a served or scheduled session.

rows.definition: model.SessionFromPrefix starts a model.Session whose KV cache is an independent clone of an already-computed prefix cache, so a known prefix can be spliced into a new session before prefilling a suffix.
rows.distinction: Not NewBackendSession (fresh empty KV on a HAL device): SessionFromPrefix is the prefix-reuse clone path used by radixkv, copying rather than starting blank.

rows.definition: gateway.SessionListResponse is the wire result of GET /v1/fak/sessions — a snapshot array of every live session's DRIVE state plus a Count.
rows.distinction: Not SessionState (one session): it is the plural fleet snapshot DTO that the CLI ps/tui panes decode.

rows.definition: sessionQ4KKernel (with siblings sessionQ8Kernel and sessionQ4Kernel) is an allocation-light per-token decode matvec kernel that wraps a *model.Session and dispatches projections by resident quant format (Q4_K / Q8 / Q4_0), reusing the session's activation buffer.
rows.distinction: Not BatchSession (multi-lane decode): these wrap ONE session for a single decode position and differ only by the weight quantization they multiply.

rows.definition: cmdSession is the `fak session` CLI entry point, delegating to its testable core runSession to read and POST DRIVE-state controls to a gateway's session REST surface.
rows.distinction: Not controlSession (the gateway-side host hook): cmdSession is the CLIENT command in cmd/fak that issues HTTP requests against that surface.

rows.definition: sessionClient is the cmd/fak HTTP client struct (base URL, key, http.Client) that the session/ps/signal/tui commands use to call the gateway's session observe/list/control endpoints.
rows.distinction: Not cmdSession (the verb dispatcher): sessionClient is the transport object the verb constructs to actually talk to the gateway.

rows.definition: gateway.SessionResetDirective is the machine-readable handoff a supervisor receives when a served session's budget is exhausted, carrying the continuation id and carryover so a fresh window can resume.
rows.distinction: Not resetServedSessionOnBudget (the host closure that performs the reset): this is the JSON payload embedded in the 409 refusal/MCP response describing the reset.

rows.definition: defaultSessionAddr returns the gateway base URL the cmd/fak session/ps/signal/tui commands target — $FAK_ADDR when set, otherwise the built-in localhost default.
rows.distinction: Not sessionClient (the client object): defaultSessionAddr only resolves the address string that client is constructed with.

rows.definition: gateway.SessionBudget is the wire form of internal/session.Budget, carrying TurnsLeft, TokensLeft, and ContextTokensLeft for one session.
rows.distinction: Not the internal session.Budget it mirrors: SessionBudget is the gateway DTO crossing the HTTP boundary, keeping the gateway session-internals-blind.

rows.definition: gateway.SessionPace is the wire form of internal/session.Pace, carrying MaxTokensPerTurn and MinTurnGapMs (zero on either axis means unset).
rows.distinction: Not SessionBudget (the run/token allotment): SessionPace governs throttling — per-turn token cap and inter-turn gap — not how much budget remains.

rows.definition: writeSessionRefusal emits the HTTP 409 a proxied request receives when an operator has held, paused, or stopped a session, embedding the SessionState reason and any reset directive.
rows.distinction: Not sessionAdmits (the decision): writeSessionRefusal is the response writer invoked once that gate or beginServedSessionTurn has already decided to refuse.

rows.definition: emitSessionJSON is the cmd/fak helper that marshals a session value (state, list, or ack) to JSON on stdout and returns the process exit code for the --json output mode.
rows.distinction: Not SessionListResponse (the data): emitSessionJSON is the renderer that serializes such a value, used across ps/signal/session verbs.

rows.definition: modelengine.NativeScheduler is the continuous-batching-shaped LifecycleEngine stub whose single scheduler loop owns one model.BatchSession and advances all admitted lanes in lockstep.
rows.distinction: Not the session.Scheduler (DRIVE-state slot picker): NativeScheduler is the decode-side engine loop that batches token generation, not the run-state admission scheduler.

rows.definition: session.Scheduler.OnSlotFreed registers the single host callback the scheduler invokes once per slot-freed SlotEvent when a session transitions to a slot-releasing run-state.
rows.distinction: Not the slot itself: OnSlotFreed is the registration seam for reacting to a freed slot, holding exactly one observer.

rows.definition: Server.ReclaimKVOnSlotFreed is the gateway's consumer of a scheduler SlotFreed event that, on a terminal cause (draining/stopped), drives the injected KV reclaimer to free that session's real KV-cache pages.
rows.distinction: Not OnSlotFreed (the scheduler-side registration): ReclaimKVOnSlotFreed is the gateway-side consumer that turns the wire-neutral SlotFreed into actual KV reclamation.

rows.definition: Server.PublishSessionRevision records one drive-state revision onto the gateway's session change feed (#630) so /v1/fak/session/changes subscribers can drain DRIVE-state updates.
rows.distinction: Not observeSession (a point-read of current state): PublishSessionRevision appends to a monotonically-revisioned stream the host pushes into the gateway.

rows.definition: serveSessions is the process-local, TraceID-keyed, bounded-LRU session.Table holding every served session's DRIVE state (run-state/budget/priority/pace), shared by the gateway session routes and any in-process agent loop.
rows.distinction: Not the gateway server: the gateway holds serveSessions only by injected closure (observe/decide/debit), never by import, so it stays session-internals-blind.

rows.definition: Server.sessionAdmits is the gateway's fallback proxy admission gate that reports whether a proxied request may proceed under the operator-controlled DRIVE run-state, failing OPEN when no ObserveSession hook is wired.
rows.distinction: Not decideSession (the mutating boundary): sessionAdmits is the observe-only fallback used when only ObserveSession is wired, debiting no budget.

rows.definition: session.SlotCause is the closed enum naming WHY a scheduling slot freed (budget-exhausted | paused | draining | stopped), carried on a SlotEvent and rendered as a lowercase token.
rows.distinction: Not the slot (the capacity unit): SlotCause is the reason annotation on a slot-freed event that the gateway keys its KV-reclaim edge on.

rows.definition: toGatewaySessionState projects an internal/session.State value into the gateway's wire SessionState DTO so cmd/fak can publish DRIVE state without the gateway importing internal/session.
rows.distinction: Not SessionState (the DTO type): toGatewaySessionState is the host-side mapping function that fills it from the internal table record.

rows.definition: agent.WithSessionTable is the RunOption that threads a per-session DRIVE-state session.Table plus the run's trace id into the agent loop so each turn honors the same budget/pace/run-state as a served request.
rows.distinction: Not serveSessions (the table instance): WithSessionTable is the option that BINDS a table+trace into an in-process agent run.

rows.definition: Server.handleFakSession is the gateway HTTP handler for /v1/fak/session/{id} — the read-write DRIVE-state control route that fans GET to observeSession and POST verbs to controlSession/steerSession.
rows.distinction: Not controlSession (the injected host hook it calls): handleFakSession is the route layer that parses the request and maps refusals to 409/400/422.
